mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor market conditions signal strong aggregate demand and wage growth, which historically precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE remaining below 5.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates synchronized supply-side capacity expansion and demand-side confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when added to a single-proxy baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates compress equity multiples, but this negative effect is fully offset when real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, resulting in neutral-to-positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain significantly negative when FEDFUNDS rises and GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the interaction effect disappears in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 3.0% year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive Fed policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where CPI YoY exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where consumer credit outstanding declines while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals credit market stress and reduced household liquidity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS decline and high FEDFUNDS regime fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production growth slows below 1.0% while unemployment remains below 5.0%, supply bottlenecks and wage-push inflation compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances matching the trigger and regime conditions, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth is added as a control variable.
